summary: A student can attach any upload from the same course to their submission by supplying that upload_id, without proving ownership or that the file was intended for that submission. This allows unauthorized reuse and rebinding of arbitrary course files in the submission workflow.

evidence_snippets.reason: Attachment validation checks only that the upload exists in the same course. It does not verify upload ownership, submission ownership, or whether the file is eligible to be attached by the current user.
The submission create paths expose the full course upload list to the student and accept any same-course upload_id as an attachment.
The submission edit paths continue to allow rebinding to any upload in the course rather than to a user-owned or submission-scoped upload.

evidence_snippets.code:
<form method="post" action="{{ url_for('assignments.create_submission_submit', course_id=course['course_id'], assignment_id=assignment['assignment_id']) }}">
  ...
  <label for="attachment_upload_id">Attachment Upload</label>
  <select id="attachment_upload_id" name="attachment_upload_id">
    <option value="">No attachment</option>
    {% for upload in uploads %}
    <option value="{{ upload['upload_id'] }}">{{ upload['original_name'] }}</option>
    {% endfor %}
  </select>
evidence_snippets.reason: The submission form presents all course uploads as selectable attachments to any course member.

root_cause: The submission workflow enforces only a same-course existence check for attachment_upload_id and does not enforce an ownership, visibility, or submission-scoped binding rule for attached files.

patch_locations.function_name: submit_assignment_page / create_submission_submit / api_create_submission / update_submission_submit / api_update_submission / edit_submission_page

repair_plan: Bind attachments to a clear authorization rule. Before accepting attachment_upload_id, verify that the current user is allowed to use that upload for this submission, such as by requiring uploader ownership, explicit staff approval, or a dedicated submission-upload mapping. The submission forms should expose only uploads that satisfy that policy instead of the entire course upload set.
